name: Rathopper
tips: Leap lets Rathopper jump over one enemy minion when moving forward. Use this to bypass a tanky blocker and threaten sacrifice on the back row.
If every tile ahead of Rathopper is occupied by enemies, Leap becomes a sacrifice — Rathopper hops over all of them and scores. This is a surprise win condition.
Rathopper cannot leap over friendly minions. If one of your own minions blocks the tile ahead, Rathopper is stuck — clear your own lane first.
At 19🗡️/13🤍, Rathopper hits hard but dies easily. Treat it as a glass cannon or sacrifice threat, not a sustained fighter.

A leap sacrifice still counts as a sacrifice and opens the opponent's react window. Note: Sparkfed Surgebot's free-deploy react needs the opponent's board to be empty — and a leap sacrifice means their lane was packed with minions, so it can't punish a leap sacrifice. Save that worry for normal back-row sacrifices.
rulings: Leap only activates when the immediate forward tile is blocked by an ENEMY minion. Ally blockers do NOT enable leap.
Leap clears at most one enemy: jumping the blocker lands Rathopper up to 2 tiles past its original position. It can never leap over two or more enemies in one move.
If all tiles from Rathopper to the opponent's back row are enemy-occupied, leap escalates to a sacrifice — Rathopper is consumed and the opponent takes damage.
The on_move trigger fires AFTER the leap lands — any ON_MOVE effects evaluate from the new position.
